message:
fix(http2): split max_frame_size into recv vs peer limits

The single max_frame_size variable was used for both reading incoming
frames (where the server's local limit applies) and writing outgoing
frames (where the peer's advertised SETTINGS_MAX_FRAME_SIZE applies).
When the peer advertised a larger frame size, read_frame() accepted
frames up to that size even though RFC 7540 Section 4.2 says an
endpoint should enforce its own receive limit.

Split into two distinct variables:
- recv_max_frame_size: our fixed local receive limit (16 KiB default),
  used for all read_frame() calls and for the h2_fc_clamp_send flow
  control read loop
- peer_max_frame_size: the peer's advertised limit from SETTINGS,
  updated by apply_http2_settings_with_fc, used only for sizing
  outgoing HEADERS/DATA frames

Applied across both the process_connection_http2 (App path) and
TcpServer (Handler/Closure paths), including the response writer
functions which now accept both limits as separate parameters.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
